name: Pacín River Valley Waterfalls Trail
name_original: Sierra de Queguas – circular por Pozo dos Cirolos y Pozo Caído
description: Hike the moderate Sierra de Queguas circular route to discover waterfalls, diverse forests, and panoramic views in Baixa Limia-Serra do Xuré
region_intro: Hiking around Entrimo offers access to a diverse landscape within the Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region is characterized by granite rock formations, varied forests, and numerous rivers and natural pools. Mountains like Serra do Quinxo and Serra Queguas define the terrain, with elevations ranging from approximately 450 meters to 1200 meters. This mountainous environment provides extensive panoramic views for hikers. Best hiking trails around Entrimo The most popular hiking route is Pacín River Valley Waterfalls Trail, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route showcases numerous waterfalls and natural pools within picturesque oak forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Geira and Muleteers' Trail, a difficult 12.4 miles (19.9 km) path. This historic route is known for its extensive mountain views and takes around 5 hours 53 minutes. Local hikers also love the Ponte da Cava da Velha – Bico do Patelo loop from Castro Laboreiro e Lamas de Mouro, a 12.9 miles (20.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 6 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Entrimo is defined by granite mountains, dense forests, and river valleys with natural pools. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging treks. The routes in Entrimo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 2800 hikers have used komoot to explore Entrimo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Entrimo?
A: Entrimo, located within the Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park, features a diverse landscape. You'll encounter dramatic granite rock formations, varied forests, and numerous rivers and natural pools. The mountainous terrain, with elevations ranging from 450 to 1200 meters, includes prominent sierras like Serra do Quinxo and Serra Queguas, offering extensive panoramic views.

Q: Are there many hiking trails available in Entrimo?
A: Yes, Entrimo offers a wide selection of hiking routes. There are over 80 trails available, catering to various fitness levels. This includes approximately 29 easy routes, 49 moderate routes, and 8 more challenging treks.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls or natural pools for swimming?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its abundant water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977169532/pacin-river-valley-waterfalls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pacín River Valley Waterfalls Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, showcasing numerous waterfalls like Os Cirolos and Pozo Caído, along with natural pools perfect for a wild swim. The Pozas de Mallón are another popular spot, offering tranquil wooded settings with waterfalls and clear waters for bathing.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites to explore along the trails?
A: Yes, Entrimo is rich in history. You can explore ancient sites such as the Castro dos Castelos, an ancient settlement, and the Pía da Moura, a former strategic fortress, both offering panoramic views. Other ancient sites, including the Dolmen Casa da Moura and anthropomorphic tombs, also dot the landscape, hinting at the region's prehistoric past.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Entrimo?
A: The routes in Entrimo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty, the variety of landscapes, and the well-maintained paths that lead through forests, past waterfalls, and to impressive viewpoints.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in the area?
A: For those seeking a longer adventure, the extensive GR50 - Grande Rota Peneda-Gerês traverses the Peneda-Gerês National Park, which borders Entrimo. This route covers approximately 190 km across 19 stages, offering a comprehensive exploration of the region's mountain ranges.

Q: What are some unique natural landmarks I can see?
A: Entrimo boasts several distinctive natural landmarks. Keep an eye out for Pica da Illa or Pedras Cabaleiras, a unique nine-meter-high rock formation in the Montes do Quinxo, notable for its precariously balanced granite blocks. Another distinctive feature is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3557474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bico do Patelo&lt;/a&gt;, a granite rock formation that serves as a natural landmark.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Entrimo?
A: Yes, many trails in Entrimo are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977169532/pacin-river-valley-waterfalls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pacín River Valley Waterfalls Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route, offering a complete experience of its natural features without retracing your steps.

Q: What other attractions are near the hiking trails in Entrimo?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore traditional hamlets like Pousios with their stone houses and ancient terraces. Nearby attractions include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/671574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Nossa Senhora da Peneda&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/672021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Laboreiro Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3482104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soajo Granaries&lt;/a&gt;. You might also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Caldo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; an interesting natural monument to visit.

Q: Is Entrimo well-connected by public transport for hikers?
A: While Entrimo is a rural area, public transport options might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance, especially if you plan to rely on public transport to reach trailheads. Many visitors find it more convenient to access the region by car, which also offers flexibility for exploring various starting points.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Entrimo suitable for families with children?
A: Entrimo offers a range of trails, including easier options that can be suitable for families. While some routes are challenging, there are also shorter, less strenuous paths that allow families to enjoy the natural beauty. Look for trails with lower elevation gain and shorter distances if hiking with children.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Entrimo?
A: The best time to hike in Entrimo is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant, with blooming flora in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations.
tips: Beautiful and leafy trail in the oak forest of Coto das Pedras in the Sierra de Queguas.

Spectacular Baroque architectural work completed in the mid-18th century located in the town of A Terrachá. The temple stands out for its monumental stone façade.
The complex is made up of three naves and a transept in the central nave, covered with a dome.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de Queguas circular trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact circular route are not provided, trails in the Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park often have designated parking areas near trailheads in villages like Queguas or Entrimo. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival or consult a detailed map for the most convenient starting point.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for all fitness levels?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It requires good physical fitness and sure-footedness, as the terrain can be varied, including rocky sections and river paths. While some shorter segments in the area might be easier, the full circular trail is more demanding than a beginner-level hike.

faqs.question: What kind of landscapes and terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse landscapes, from lush oak, holm oak, and wild olive forests to open meadows and rocky areas with prominent granite formations. You'll traverse river paths and stone roads, with sections offering spectacular panoramic views of the Sierra do Xurés and even the Peneda-Gerês National Park in Portugal.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Sierra de Queguas circular route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the beautiful Pozo Caído Waterfall, the series of pools and small waterfalls at Pozo dos Cirolos, and the serene Coto das Pedras Oak Forest Trail. You might also pass by the historic Church of Santa María a Real de A Terrachá and potentially ancient shepherd constructions or megalithic dolmens.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: It's common to spot wild horses and native "Cachena" cows grazing freely in the area, adding to the unique charm of the Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park generally offers pleasant hiking conditions in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially at higher altitudes, while winter might bring colder weather and potentially more challenging conditions on some sections.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Queguas circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park generally allows dogs on trails. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially due to the presence of wild horses and Cachena cows, and to protect the local wildlife and environment.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail within the Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park. However, as it's a protected UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, visitors are expected to respect all natural park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Caminho da Geira e dos Arrieiros, Camiño dos Soutos Vellos, Camiño das Serpes, Camiño da Estalada, Pista da Barroca, and Camiño da Montaña.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is primarily natural, so dedicated facilities or potable water sources are not common directly on the path. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks. You might find cafes, pubs, or accommodation in nearby villages like Queguas or Entrimo before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This specific route, "Sierra de Queguas – circular via Pozo dos Cirolos and Pozo Caído," is a circular trail, meaning it forms a loop and brings you back to your starting point without retracing your steps.

waypoints.name: Coto das Pedras Oak Forest Trail
